description: Allows active duty to earn Department of Labor apprenticeship certifications while performing regular military duties. No additional training required — your job IS the training.
eligibility: Active duty Navy, Marine Corps, and Coast Guard. Must be in an eligible rating/MOS.
how_to_apply: Register on USMAP website. Select apprenticeship trade matching your rating. Log hours as you work. Receive DoL Journeyman certificate upon completion.
key_benefits: No additional training — log hours from regular duties
limitations: Must complete required hours (typically 2,000-8,000 depending on trade). Only Navy, USMC, and USCG currently participate.

description: Allows active duty service members to participate in civilian job training, internships, or apprenticeships during their last 180 days of service while still receiving military pay and benefits.
eligibility: Active duty within last 180 days of service. Command approval required. Must have honorable service.
how_to_apply: Find approved SkillBridge program. Get command approval. Participate while drawing full military pay/benefits. Many programs lead directly to job offers.
key_benefits: Continue receiving military pay and benefits
Many programs lead to direct hire
limitations: Command approval required. Must be within 180 days of separation. Not all commands approve. Mission requirements may limit availability.

description: Coast Guard COOL funds certifications for active duty Coast Guard members.
eligibility: Active duty Coast Guard. Must be in a rating eligible for the requested credential.
how_to_apply: Log into USCG COOL with CAC. Search by rating. Select credential. Submit request.
limitations: Must be active duty Coast Guard.

description: Air Force COOL funds certifications and licenses for active duty Airmen and Guardians. Covers exam fees and prep materials.
eligibility: Active duty Air Force and Space Force. Must be in an AFSC eligible for the requested credential.
how_to_apply: Access AF COOL through the AFVEC portal. Search by AFSC. Select credential. Submit funding request through supervisor.
limitations: Must be active duty. AFSC must be eligible for requested credential.

description: Navy COOL funds industry certifications and licenses for active duty Sailors. Covers exam fees, prep materials, and some training costs.
eligibility: Active duty Navy enlisted and officers. Must be in a rating/designator eligible for the requested credential.
how_to_apply: Log into Navy COOL website with CAC. Search for your rating. Select credential. Submit funding request through the portal. Command approval required.
limitations: Must be active duty. Credential must be related to your rating or career goals. Some certifications have waiting lists.

description: VA program for veterans with service-connected disabilities. Covers training, education, certifications, tools, and job placement support.
eligibility: Veterans with service-connected disability rating (typically 10%+) and an employment barrier. Active duty with expected medical separation also eligible.
how_to_apply: Apply through VA.gov. Meet with VR&E counselor. Develop individualized rehabilitation plan.
key_benefits: Provides tools and equipment needed for employment
Can be used after GI Bill is exhausted
limitations: Must have service-connected disability and employment barrier. Counselor determines eligibility. 48-month entitlement limit.

program_name: Defense Activity for Non-Traditional Education Support
description: Funds CLEP and DSST exams for active duty military. Earn college credits for knowledge gained through military experience — for free.
eligibility: Active duty military (all branches). Some benefits extend to eligible family members.
how_to_apply: Visit your base Education Center. Register for CLEP/DSST exam. Take exam at approved testing center on base.
key_benefits: CLEP and DSST exams are free for active duty
Earn college credits without taking classes
limitations: Active duty only for free exams. Veterans pay $90 per CLEP exam. Not all colleges accept all CLEP credits — verify first.

description: Army CA (formerly Army COOL) funds industry certifications and licenses for active duty Soldiers. Up to $4,000 per fiscal year.
eligibility: Active duty Army enlisted and officers. Must have commander approval.
how_to_apply: Access Army COOL/CA through ArmyIgnitED portal. Select credential. Get commander approval. Submit voucher request.
limitations: Annual funding cap of $4,000. Must be active duty. Commander approval required.

program_name: Veteran Employment Through Technology Education Courses
description: VA program that pays for high-tech training programs WITHOUT using your GI Bill benefits. Covers coding bootcamps, cybersecurity training, IT courses, and more. Includes housing allowance during training.
eligibility: Veterans with at least one day of unexpired GI Bill benefits (does not consume your GI Bill). Must be accepted into a VA-approved VET TEC program.
how_to_apply: Apply through VA.gov for VET TEC. Separately apply to a VA-approved training provider. VA matches you with provider.
key_benefits: Does NOT use your GI Bill months
Covers cybersecurity, coding, data, IT training
Programs range from weeks to months
limitations: Must have at least 1 day of GI Bill eligibility remaining (but does not consume it). Limited approved providers. Competitive — apply early.

description: Covers tuition, fees, housing allowance, and books for degree programs, vocational training, and some certification programs. The most comprehensive education benefit.
eligibility: Veterans with at least 90 days of active duty service after 9/10/2001. Benefits scale based on time served (40%-100%). Dependents may use transferred benefits.
how_to_apply: Apply through VA.gov. Obtain Certificate of Eligibility (COE). Enroll in VA-approved program. School certifies enrollment.
key_benefits: Full tuition at public schools (or Yellow Ribbon at private)
limitations: 36 months of benefits total. Must be VA-approved program. Housing allowance based on school zip code. Cannot use simultaneously with other VA education benefits.

description: Marine Corps COOL funds certifications and licenses for active duty Marines. Covers exam fees and some prep materials.
eligibility: Active duty Marines. Must be in an MOS eligible for the requested credential.
how_to_apply: Log into USMC COOL with CAC. Search by MOS. Select credential. Submit funding request.
limitations: Must be active duty. Credential must relate to MOS or transition goals.

description: Federal grant for undergraduate education based on financial need. Can be used alongside GI Bill. Does not need to be repaid.
eligibility: Must demonstrate financial need via FAFSA. Available to undergraduate students who have not earned a bachelor's degree.
how_to_apply: Complete FAFSA at studentaid.gov. School determines eligibility and applies grant to tuition.
key_benefits: Can be combined with GI Bill
Does not need to be repaid
Pocket the difference if GI Bill covers tuition
limitations: Based on financial need. Only for undergraduate programs. Must complete FAFSA annually.

description: Free career training and industry certification program through Syracuse University IVMF. Covers training and exam costs for in-demand certifications across IT, cybersecurity, project management, and more.
eligibility: Active duty service members (any branch, E-1 through O-6), veterans (within 1 year of separation), and military spouses.
how_to_apply: Apply online at ivmf.syracuse.edu. Select certification track. Complete online coursework. Take certification exam (paid by program).
key_benefits: 100% free — training AND exam fees covered
Available to active duty, veterans, and spouses
Covers PMP, CISSP, AWS, CompTIA, Scrum, and many more
limitations: Must apply and be accepted. Limited seats per cohort. Veterans must be within 1 year of separation (some exceptions). Active duty needs command approval.

description: Monthly education benefit for veterans who opted in during service. Fixed monthly payment regardless of tuition cost.
eligibility: Veterans who elected and paid into MGIB during service ($100/month for 12 months). Must have honorable discharge.
how_to_apply: Apply through VA.gov. Monthly payments sent directly to veteran.
key_benefits: Can be used for degree programs, vocational, flight training
limitations: Lower benefit than Post-9/11 in most cases. Can convert to Post-9/11 if eligible.

description: Federal job training program available through American Job Centers. Can fund certifications, training programs, and career services for eligible veterans.
eligibility: Veterans receive priority of service at American Job Centers. Must be registered with local workforce center.
how_to_apply: Visit local American Job Center. Register as veteran (priority service). Work with career counselor to identify approved training programs.
limitations: Funding varies by state and local area. Must work through American Job Center. Some programs have waiting lists.
